clean_body: Democrats are outraising us, and they're voting in elections that, to put it simply, we're not voting in. Recently, voter complacency in Iowa cost Republicans a super-majority in the Iowa Senate. | Peter, The partisan divide gripping our nation has turned Washington into a warzone of its own—fierce, unrelenting, and filled with entrenched elites more interested in power than progress. As a Navy SEAL, I've faced harsh environments and impossible odds, from battlefields abroad to the political arena at home. Those experiences taught me how to stay calm under pressure, broker compromise, and accomplish the mission—no matter the stakes. I'm Sen. Tim Sheehy, and I'm in the U.S. Senate to serve you, not the special interests or partisan extremists. The liberal media and their allies attack me for my resolve to challenge the status quo and expose the waste and corruption draining our nation. They fear leaders who put America first and refuse to bow to their agenda. My SEAL background gives me a unique perspective:I know how to navigate chaos, build bridges, and lower temperatures in even the most hostile settings. In Congress, I'm fighting for practical solutions—securing our borders, strengthening our economy, and restoring accountability in government. But I can't do it alone. Democrats are outraising us, and they're voting in elections that, to put it simply, we're not voting in. Recently, voter complacency in Iowa cost Republicans a super-majority in the Iowa Senate. What happens when that complacency extends to U.S. House and Senate races? We lose. I can't abide by that - and I don't believe you can either - so I'm sounding the alarm right now. Peter, I need your support to keep pushing for a stronger, united America. Together, we can reject the elites' divisive tactics and build a future that honors the sacrifices of our forefathers. to fuel this fight to fuel this fight to fuel this fight The attacks from partisan outlets won't stop—they're desperate to protect their broken system. But with your help, we can stay focused on delivering results for the American people. If they silence voices like mine, they'll come for yours next. Thank you for your unwavering support. For America's Future, Tim Sheehy United States Senator - Montana | " "I SERVED AS A NAVY SEAL, BUILT ONE OF THE LARGEST AERIAL FIREFIGHTING COMPANIES IN AMERICA CREATING OVER 200 MONTANA JOBS, AND STARTED A VETERAN-LED CATTLE RANCHING AND FEEDING OPERATION. WHETHER IT WAS IN WAR OR BUSINESS, I SEE PROBLEMS AND SOLVE THEM. AMERICA NEEDS CONSERVATIVE LEADERS WHO LOVE OUR COUNTRY. I'M RUNNING FOR THE U.S. SENATE BECAUSE OUR CAMPAIGN IS ABOUT SERVICE, DUTY, AND COUNTRY -- NOT POLITICS AS USUAL." | PAID FOR BY TIM SHEEHY FOR MONTANA Tim Sheehy is a former Navy SEAL. name: Andrew Sypher, Turning Point USA
subject: 121,000+ reasons to be hopeful
body:
| [View in Web Browser](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e10690744/VaHR0cHM6Ly93cm8ubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3YvRW5nVWY2dzJYcA/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTEwMDQ/LbmNsQjcwSTcxbg/qP2hpZHM9bmN5Z0JTOTVwQzZyTTBscw/gaOFJ2w/s9r1d4dec4b)
| |
---
Patriot, We knew Charlie’s mission would live on. _But we didn’t expect this..._ In just two weeks, more than 121,000 students have reached out to start or join a Turning Point USA chapter at their college or high school.
---
[](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
Young Americans — from high schools to college campuses — are raising their hands and saying: ** _“I’m ready to lead.”_** And thanks to you — many of them already are. Patriot, your prayers... your generosity... You’ve helped us meet this moment with activism kits, leadership resources, and boldness. Charlie dreamed of a Turning Point USA chapter in every college and high school in America. Now, it’s within reach.
---
[](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
Our movement was already strong, but no one has ever seen anything like this before... And it’s moving faster than anyone expected. From high school students launching Club America chapters to college students organizing with urgency... **The youth movement Charlie built is gaining unstoppable momentum.** **And Patriot, behind every new chapter... is someone like you — making sure students have what they need to lead.** We are _deeply grateful_ for you. And we promise — we’re not slowing down.
---
**For Charlie,** Andrew Sypher
Vice President of Field Operations Turning Point USA
---
---
[**Support Turning Point USA**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.) **P.S.** If you’ve already helped — by sponsoring an activism kit or becoming a monthly donor — **thank you.** Your generosity is equipping students, launching chapters, and honoring Charlie’s mission in the most tangible way possible. We couldn’t do this without you. But if you haven’t had the chance yet — _and you feel called to act..._ **Here is a powerful way you can help:** **[Every $25 you give today helps stock another activism kit — including leadership tools, Constitutions, flags, and chapter gear — for a new student chapter...](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)** **Again, if you’ve already stepped forward to help —**** _thank you._** Your impact is already being felt on campuses and in high schools across America. For Charlie. For the next generation. For America.
---
[ 1 KIT = $25 ](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
[ 2 KITS = $50 ](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
[ 10 KITS = $250 ](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
[ 100 KITS = $2,500 ](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
[ 1,000 KITS = $25,000 ](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
[ Sponsor an Activism Kit ](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
4940 East Beverly Road
Phoenix, Arizona 85044
---

This email was sent to **[dpwillis67@gmail.com](mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com)** as part of your subscription to **America First War Room**

108 S Washington St. Suite 200
Alexandria, VA 22314

I don’t mess around with something as important as representing the people of my home, Peter. Every race I’ve ever run, I’ve been in it to win — even when the odds seemed long. You’ve been there with me every time, and together, we’ve succeeded. Now, this election for the U.S. Senate may be our toughest challenge yet. So [**when I ask you to donate to our campaign, it’s because your contribution**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/116586696/573894023/-1743631236?refcode=ea.70aecc60-40a1-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ms=ea.70aecc60-40a1-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MGFlY2M2MC00MGExLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=g5ZxXSTEC1JAntVMY_HKO1DfCbw58HR9AAkxFzR_yaE=) could make the difference between victory and defeat. **Republicans see me as the top threat to their Senate majority. They know that we have the team, the support, and the momentum to win.** That means national attention will be on us. I will always be straightforward about this campaign: our plans, where we stand in the polls, what we expect to come, and what we need from you. Right now, we’re preparing for national GOP money groups to spend _hundreds of millions_ against us, and Democrats are being outraised by Republicans. Meanwhile, various polls have shown either a dead heat or us with a slight lead. **[I am asking you to contribute just $5 or another amount that fits in your budget today because we can’t afford to leave this race up to chance — or up to Republicans’ deep-pocketed donors and their dark-money groups. We can win this race, but ONLY if we stick together. Please, give what you can today.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/116586697/573894024/-1743631236?amount=5&refcode=ea.70aecc60-40a1-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ms=ea.70aecc60-40a1-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MGFlY2M2MC00MGExLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=g5ZxXSTEC1JAntVMY_HKO1DfCbw58HR9AAkxFzR_yaE=)** | **[DONATE $5](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/116586698/573894025/-1743631236?amount=5&refcode=ea.70aecc60-40a1-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ms=ea.70aecc60-40a1-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MGFlY2M2MC00MGExLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=g5ZxXSTEC1JAntVMY_HKO1DfCbw58HR9AAkxFzR_yaE=)**

Roy Cooper

Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here.

National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us.

Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party.

Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy.

Thank you for your support,
Team Deb
P.S. We know the Republican shutdown is impacting tens of thousands of New Mexicans and folks across the country. If you’re not able to donate today, we completely understand.

In the late summer of 2025, Oracle's stock surged 25%, propelled by a single announcement: a $60 billion annual deal to provide cloud computing for OpenAI, the creator of ChatGPT.
The catch?
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/535f7325-1126-4547-b3d3-1304db8ed0ea?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Michael Cembalest, the Chairman of Market and Investment Strategy for J.P. Morgan Asset & Wealth Management encapsulated it in a nutshell.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/4e182151-da12-4856-a53a-3639e42a9e29?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_**" Oracle's stock jumped by 25% after being promised $60 billion a year from OpenAI, an amount of money OpenAI doesn't earn yet, to provide cloud computing facilities that Oracle hasn't built yet, and which will require 4.5 GW of power (the equivalent of 2.25 Hoover Dams or four nuclear plants), as well as increased borrowing by Oracle whose debt to equity ratio is already 500% compared to 50% for Amazon, 30% for Microsoft and even less at Meta and Google. **_
_**In other words, the tech capital cycle may be about to change. "**_
Cembalest's comment that this deal signals a shift in the tech capital cycle is one that eerily recalls the speculative fervor of the dot-com era, a warning of sorts.
* * *
* * *
**[Together with Next Thing Technologies](https://substack.com/redirect/74304ecf-9205-40a8-a30c-f2f4938b159f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
# **[NVIDIA's $46.74B Earnings Prove AI Is Here to Stay...And This Can Power It All](https://substack.com/redirect/74304ecf-9205-40a8-a30c-f2f4938b159f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/74304ecf-9205-40a8-a30c-f2f4938b159f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
NVIDIA shocked Wall Street again with **$46.74 billion in revenue** , up **56% year over year.** Their CFO now expects**$3 -$4 trillion in AI infrastructure spending by 2030.**1 Oracle's results tell the same story.
The company reported**$14.9 billion in revenue, up 12% year over year.** Cloud infrastructure sales jumped**55%** , while long-term cloud contracts surged to**$455 billion -- a 359% increase in just one year.**
The message couldn't be clearer: **AI isn 't a bubble. It's the new economy.**1
**The Problem No One Talks About:**
Data centers and AI supercomputers run 24/7, devouring electricity.
But **lithium batteries can 't keep up**.1 They're too expensive, supplies are tight, and**China controls more than 80% of global lithium processing.**
If AI is going to scale to trillions of dollars, the real constraint isn't chips...
It's power storage.1
**The Breakthrough That Could Change Everything**
That's why **sodium-ion batteries** are emerging as the solution.1
* Sodium is**1,400x more abundant** **than lithium** , with significant reserves located in the U.S9
* Batteries can be made at a **fraction of the cost of lithium -- up to 90% less expensive in the first year of use.6**
* They last longer8, are safer7, and can be built at scale using domestic materials.1

And **Next Thing Technologies** , an American-owned company, is bringing this breakthrough to market. Their sodium-ion batteries are designed to power everything from **massive AI data centers** to **affordable home backup systems**.1
Think about it: NVIDIA builds the chips.
Oracle runs the cloud. But without safer, cheaper, U.S.-made batteries, the AI boom hits a wall.
Next Thing Technologies is building the infrastructure that could make it possible.1
Already, **7,800+ investors have committed more than $7 million. 3** And right now, [shares are available at just ](https://substack.com/redirect/74304ecf-9205-40a8-a30c-f2f4938b159f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[$6 with up to 20% in bonus shares.4](https://substack.com/redirect/74304ecf-9205-40a8-a30c-f2f4938b159f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**

## Are we witnessing the birth of a transformative industry or the inflation of a dangerous bubble?
The AI industry's meteoric rise feels both exhilarating and unnervingly familiar. AI Backbone companies like OpenAI, Nvidia, and Oracle are betting billions on a future where artificial intelligence reshapes entire economies and societies. Yet, beneath the glossy projections, a circular economy could be emerging--one where tech giants cycle investment deals among themselves, and where valuations are based on promises of growth, that for now, remain speculative.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/4fe245e8-a5b4-4b6e-85d4-67e9bf9fc004?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
In certain ways this dynamic mirrors the dot-com boom, when companies like Nortel Networks soared on the hype of future internet infrastructure only to collapse when the math didn't add up. Nortel's market cap peaked at $398 billion in 2000, **accounting for a third of Canada 's stock market,** before **[plummeting](https://substack.com/redirect/2d3f58e0-6880-429a-8ca9-df538720d9b8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** to near-zero by 2002.
The brutal lesson was clear: unchecked optimism, fueled by tantalizing theoretical profits, can lead to catastrophic reckonings.
Analyst's have projected that generative AI could add $2.6 trillion to $4.4 trillion to the global economy by 2030, but only if companies can translate adoption into scalable revenue models.
OpenAI's valuation has soared. From $29 Billion in 2023 to its valuation **[this week of $500 Billion](https://substack.com/redirect/4157eb91-a666-42d7-9bb0-0233b6434861?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** making it the **[most valuable privately held company ever.](https://substack.com/redirect/58bea684-8627-48d5-8ff9-241ea7d8b37f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/7605b71a-4161-4fe8-96a6-5dca8167d8cd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
In light of the above table, consider OpenAI's deal with Oracle. The $60 billion figure is eye-popping, but OpenAI's revenue streams--primarily from subscriptions and enterprise licensing--fall far short of justifying such a commitment. Despite ChatGPT's global ubiquity, with millions using AI tools daily, the raw monetization of generative AI, for the moment, remains embryonic. This is not to say that it won't grow to eventually fund that $60 Billion a year annual deal with Oracle but right now its embryonic.
This disconnect raises a question: how sustainable (or stable) is a business model built on promises rather than profits?
Nvidia, another pillar of the AI boom, exemplifies this paradox. The company's chips power the data centers driving AI innovation, and **[its revenue has soared.](https://substack.com/redirect/e5358e2c-dcf4-44b9-8753-6bc4441a171a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[ ](https://substack.com/redirect/e5358e2c-dcf4-44b9-8753-6bc4441a171a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)Yet for sometime now, **[whispers](https://substack.com/redirect/f781a37c-6b7a-45c8-960e-40f6e592a065?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** surround its sales. Industry analysts, such as **[@JustDario](https://substack.com/redirect/0b58ee1c-9d52-4e7e-ac2d-0cbe58c84fac?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** on X, have questioned who exactly is **[buying these chips](https://substack.com/redirect/36ecda7a-7c01-4f13-802f-f1d319a9f07a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** and **[how those deals are structured](https://substack.com/redirect/258080ef-0237-43eb-b539-5e2c975d0483?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**.
These **[details are murky](https://substack.com/redirect/1cb3fc4e-3456-4723-9060-5bbcc9fb49f3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** at best and in some cases **[downright opaque](https://substack.com/redirect/dd8ed261-c74d-4e10-ac98-aecfa397b5e3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**. Are tech giants stockpiling hardware in anticipation of demand, or is this a game of pass-the-debt, where **[purchases are financed](https://substack.com/redirect/7e0e36d8-c96d-4534-8161-de97afc45f3b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** by funds borrowed against inflated valuations?
This lack of transparency harks back to the dot-com era's frenzy, when companies like Pets.com burned through cash on unproven business models, only to fold when investors demanded results.
* * *
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2b27df5b-cf68-4a8f-95c5-987c9544cdbb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
# Or not?
The scale of OpenAI and Oracle's ambitions are most starkly illustrated **[by its energy demands](https://substack.com/redirect/52cf5d3c-28e8-418f-9d9b-fb077cca1f30?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** and it is only when those numbers are examined that the scale of the task ahead rears its head.
* Oracle's proposed data centers would require 4.5 gigawatts of power, enough to supply power to 3 million homes.
* For context, Cembalest references the **[Hoover Dam](https://substack.com/redirect/b513093e-e9e4-4df8-9ec7-43e84b36ecf6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , which powers 1.3 million homes, generates roughly 2 gigawatts annually (depending on water levels) and cost $900 million (in 2025 dollars) to build in the 1930s.
* Replicating that today, adjusted for modern costs, environmental regulations, and geographic suitability would be a multibillion-dollar endeavor.

Nuclear power offers a more realistic solution, but even that is daunting.
* The **[Vogtle Electric](https://substack.com/redirect/122bfb10-d543-4386-83e8-0a81d1ad15a3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** Generating Plant's recently activated Units 3 and 4 in Georgia, which cost $37 billion and together they produce 3.4 gigawatts.
* To meet Oracle's needs, an additional reactor would be needed, conservatively pushing costs toward $55 billion just to build the energy infrastructure required to feed the datacenter.
* $55B would be before accounting for building the data centers themselves or the chips to fill them.

Perhaps the power stations will be funded by the federal government which will defray the cost, but Government intervention will bring inevitable oversight and delays as it is no small thing to put up three nuclear reactors. Also, there are already questions being asked about the percentage of public state electricity consumed by data centers.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/144fa260-e62e-4f17-9928-a40cbb37193d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
With nearly 40% of Virginia's power now being routed to data centers, this very quickly is going to become a political issue.
* * *
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/b5b6676b-1170-483c-9176-8628221a869f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
# No seriously, show me the money?
Debt is the undercurrent that is pulling this enterprise into uncharted waters. Oracle's debt-to-equity ratio, already orders of magnitude higher than Amazon's, signals a company stretching its financial limits. OpenAI, too, despite its latest valuation which is staggering, is banking on a future where its they becomes the Google or Apple of AI for the next quarter-century.
Sam Altman's vision is certainly bold: If OpenAI truly can deliver the world's most advanced AI, today's investments will look like pocket change and he will rightfully go down in history as one of humanity's greats alongside Jobs, Tesla, Edison and Newton.
But the scale of borrowing--coupled with an as yet unproven revenue model--invites comparisons to Enron, which infamously booked theoretical future profits before they had been made (granted this was done with the complicity of their auditor Arthur Andersen.)
While no one accuses OpenAI or Oracle of Enron's criminality, their plan's total reliance on _speculative_ _gains_ raises red flags. Cembalest's quote suggests that even titans of Wall Street's are starting to feel skeptical about the viability of these grand plans.
Even when an industry is mature, titans can fall. Blackberry was once the ultimate communicator but was blown to dust by the iPhone, and the AI "market" is no where near as mature as the consumer cellular market was in 2007.
The AI boom's parallels to the dot-com era are not just financial but cultural. In the late 1990s, the internet was hailed as a transformative force, much as AI is today. Yet, the gap between vision and eventual execution proved fatal for many early companies. Nortel's collapse left thousands jobless and investors ruined, a cautionary tale of what happens when hype outpaces reality.
Today's AI enthusiasts argue that the technology's potential--its ability to revolutionize healthcare, education, and industry--justifies the risk. But potential is not profit, and the logistical hurdles of scaling AI are gargantuan. From power generation to chip supply chains, the infrastructure required demands not just capital but time, political will, and environmental trade-offs.
The AI industry could indeed reshape the world eventually, **[Jeff Bezos certainly thinks so,](https://substack.com/redirect/3770def1-b0e6-466a-9dbe-5cc7532466f5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** delivering fantastical breakthroughs that entirely justify today's gambles. But the path forward requires sobriety, not exuberance. Policymakers must grapple with the environmental and economic implications of AI's growth, while investors should demand transparency in how deals are structured and funded. For the public, the challenge is to separate genuine innovation from speculative froth. The dot-com bust taught us that not every revolution delivers on its initial promises, even if they do in the end.
As we stand on the cusp of AI's next phase, the question looms: are we building a new era of progress, or chasing shadows of a boom that cannot last?

Their CFO now expects$3 -$4 trillion in AI infrastructure spending by 2030.1 Oracle's results tell the same story. The company reported$14.9 billion in revenue, up 12% year over year. Cloud infrastructure sales jumped55% , while long-term cloud contracts surged to$455 billion -- a 359% increase in just one year. The message couldn't be clearer: AI isn 't a bubble. It's the new economy.1 The Problem No One Talks About: Data centers and AI supercomputers run 24/7, devouring electricity. But lithium batteries can 't keep up.1 They're too expensive, supplies are tight, andChina controls more than 80% of global lithium processing. If AI is going to scale to trillions of dollars, the real constraint isn't chips... It's power storage.1 The Breakthrough That Could Change Everything That's why sodium-ion batteries are emerging as the solution.1 Sodium is1,400x more abundant than lithium , with significant reserves located in the U.S9 Batteries can be made at a fraction of the cost of lithium -- up to 90% less expensive in the first year of use.6 They last longer8, are safer7, and can be built at scale using domestic materials.1 And Next Thing Technologies , an American-owned company, is bringing this breakthrough to market. Their sodium-ion batteries are designed to power everything from massive AI data centers to affordable home backup systems.1 Think about it: NVIDIA builds the chips. Oracle runs the cloud. But without safer, cheaper, U.S.-made batteries, the AI boom hits a wall. Next Thing Technologies is building the infrastructure that could make it possible.1 Already, 7,800+ investors have committed more than $7 million. 3 And right now, Are we witnessing the birth of a transformative industry or the inflation of a dangerous bubble? The AI industry's meteoric rise feels both exhilarating and unnervingly familiar. AI Backbone companies like OpenAI, Nvidia, and Oracle are betting billions on a future where artificial intelligence reshapes entire economies and societies. Yet, beneath the glossy projections, a circular economy could be emerging--one where tech giants cycle investment deals among themselves, and where valuations are based on promises of growth, that for now, remain speculative. | | ---|---|--- In certain ways this dynamic mirrors the dot-com boom, when companies like Nortel Networks soared on the hype of future internet infrastructure only to collapse when the math didn't add up. Nortel's market cap peaked at $398 billion in 2000, accounting for a third of Canada 's stock market, before to near-zero by 2002. The brutal lesson was clear: unchecked optimism, fueled by tantalizing theoretical profits, can lead to catastrophic reckonings. Analyst's have projected that generative AI could add $2.6 trillion to $4.4 trillion to the global economy by 2030, but only if companies can translate adoption into scalable revenue models. OpenAI's valuation has soared. From $29 Billion in 2023 to its valuation making it the | | ---|---|--- In light of the above table, consider OpenAI's deal with Oracle. The $60 billion figure is eye-popping, but OpenAI's revenue streams--primarily from subscriptions and enterprise licensing--fall far short of justifying such a commitment. Despite ChatGPT's global ubiquity, with millions using AI tools daily, the raw monetization of generative AI, for the moment, remains embryonic. This is not to say that it won't grow to eventually fund that $60 Billion a year annual deal with Oracle but right now its embryonic. This disconnect raises a question: how sustainable (or stable) is a business model built on promises rather than profits? Nvidia, another pillar of the AI boom, exemplifies this paradox. The company's chips power the data centers driving AI innovation, and for sometime now, surround its sales. Industry analysts, such as on X, have questioned who exactly is and . These at best and in some cases . Are tech giants stockpiling hardware in anticipation of demand, or is this a game of pass-the-debt, where by funds borrowed against inflated valuations? This lack of transparency harks back to the dot-com era's frenzy, when companies like Pets.com burned through cash on unproven business models, only to fold when investors demanded results. | | ---|---|--- Or not? The scale of OpenAI and Oracle's ambitions are most starkly illustrated and it is only when those numbers are examined that the scale of the task ahead rears its head. Oracle's proposed data centers would require 4.5 gigawatts of power, enough to supply power to 3 million homes. For context, Cembalest references the , which powers 1.3 million homes, generates roughly 2 gigawatts annually (depending on water levels) and cost $900 million (in 2025 dollars) to build in the 1930s. Replicating that today, adjusted for modern costs, environmental regulations, and geographic suitability would be a multibillion-dollar endeavor. Nuclear power offers a more realistic solution, but even that is daunting. The Generating Plant's recently activated Units 3 and 4 in Georgia, which cost $37 billion and together they produce 3.4 gigawatts. To meet Oracle's needs, an additional reactor would be needed, conservatively pushing costs toward $55 billion just to build the energy infrastructure required to feed the datacenter. $55B would be before accounting for building the data centers themselves or the chips to fill them. Perhaps the power stations will be funded by the federal government which will defray the cost, but Government intervention will bring inevitable oversight and delays as it is no small thing to put up three nuclear reactors. Also, there are already questions being asked about the percentage of public state electricity consumed by data centers. | | ---|---|--- With nearly 40% of Virginia's power now being routed to data centers, this very quickly is going to become a political issue. | | ---|---|--- No seriously, show me the money? Debt is the undercurrent that is pulling this enterprise into uncharted waters. Oracle's debt-to-equity ratio, already orders of magnitude higher than Amazon's, signals a company stretching its financial limits. OpenAI, too, despite its latest valuation which is staggering, is banking on a future where its they becomes the Google or Apple of AI for the next quarter-century. Sam Altman's vision is certainly bold: If OpenAI truly can deliver the world's most advanced AI, today's investments will look like pocket change and he will rightfully go down in history as one of humanity's greats alongside Jobs, Tesla, Edison and Newton. But the scale of borrowing--coupled with an as yet unproven revenue model--invites comparisons to Enron, which infamously booked theoretical future profits before they had been made (granted this was done with the complicity of their auditor Arthur Andersen.) While no one accuses OpenAI or Oracle of Enron's criminality, their plan's total reliance on speculative gains raises red flags. Cembalest's quote suggests that even titans of Wall Street's are starting to feel skeptical about the viability of these grand plans. Even when an industry is mature, titans can fall. Blackberry was once the ultimate communicator but was blown to dust by the iPhone, and the AI "market" is no where near as mature as the consumer cellular market was in 2007. The AI boom's parallels to the dot-com era are not just financial but cultural. In the late 1990s, the internet was hailed as a transformative force, much as AI is today. Yet, the gap between vision and eventual execution proved fatal for many early companies. Nortel's collapse left thousands jobless and investors ruined, a cautionary tale of what happens when hype outpaces reality. Today's AI enthusiasts argue that the technology's potential--its ability to revolutionize healthcare, education, and industry--justifies the risk. But potential is not profit, and the logistical hurdles of scaling AI are gargantuan. From power generation to chip supply chains, the infrastructure required demands not just capital but time, political will, and environmental trade-offs. The AI industry could indeed reshape the world eventually, delivering fantastical breakthroughs that entirely justify today's gambles. But the path forward requires sobriety, not exuberance. Policymakers must grapple with the environmental and economic implications of AI's growth, while investors should demand transparency in how deals are structured and funded. For the public, the challenge is to separate genuine innovation from speculative froth. The dot-com bust taught us that not every revolution delivers on its initial promises, even if they do in the end. As we stand on the cusp of AI's next phase, the question looms: are we building a new era of progress, or chasing shadows of a boom that cannot last? Thanks for reading The Capitalist! In the late summer of 2025, Oracle's stock surged 25%, propelled by a single announcement: a $60 billion annual deal to provide cloud computing for OpenAI, the creator of ChatGPT.
The catch?
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/535f7325-1126-4547-b3d3-1304db8ed0ea?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Michael Cembalest, the Chairman of Market and Investment Strategy for J.P. Morgan Asset & Wealth Management encapsulated it in a nutshell.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/4e182151-da12-4856-a53a-3639e42a9e29?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_**" Oracle's stock jumped by 25% after being promised $60 billion a year from OpenAI, an amount of money OpenAI doesn't earn yet, to provide cloud computing facilities that Oracle hasn't built yet, and which will require 4.5 GW of power (the equivalent of 2.25 Hoover Dams or four nuclear plants), as well as increased borrowing by Oracle whose debt to equity ratio is already 500% compared to 50% for Amazon, 30% for Microsoft and even less at Meta and Google. **_
_**In other words, the tech capital cycle may be about to change. "**_
Cembalest's comment that this deal signals a shift in the tech capital cycle is one that eerily recalls the speculative fervor of the dot-com era, a warning of sorts.
* * *
* * *
**[Together with Next Thing Technologies](https://substack.com/redirect/74304ecf-9205-40a8-a30c-f2f4938b159f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
# **[NVIDIA's $46.74B Earnings Prove AI Is Here to Stay...And This Can Power It All](https://substack.com/redirect/74304ecf-9205-40a8-a30c-f2f4938b159f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/74304ecf-9205-40a8-a30c-f2f4938b159f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
NVIDIA shocked Wall Street again with **$46.74 billion in revenue** , up **56% year over year.** Their CFO now expects**$3 -$4 trillion in AI infrastructure spending by 2030.**1 Oracle's results tell the same story.
The company reported**$14.9 billion in revenue, up 12% year over year.** Cloud infrastructure sales jumped**55%** , while long-term cloud contracts surged to**$455 billion -- a 359% increase in just one year.**
The message couldn't be clearer: **AI isn 't a bubble. It's the new economy.**1
**The Problem No One Talks About:**
Data centers and AI supercomputers run 24/7, devouring electricity.
But **lithium batteries can 't keep up**.1 They're too expensive, supplies are tight, and**China controls more than 80% of global lithium processing.**
If AI is going to scale to trillions of dollars, the real constraint isn't chips...
It's power storage.1
**The Breakthrough That Could Change Everything**
That's why **sodium-ion batteries** are emerging as the solution.1
* Sodium is**1,400x more abundant** **than lithium** , with significant reserves located in the U.S9
* Batteries can be made at a **fraction of the cost of lithium -- up to 90% less expensive in the first year of use.6**
* They last longer8, are safer7, and can be built at scale using domestic materials.1

And **Next Thing Technologies** , an American-owned company, is bringing this breakthrough to market. Their sodium-ion batteries are designed to power everything from **massive AI data centers** to **affordable home backup systems**.1
Think about it: NVIDIA builds the chips.
Oracle runs the cloud. But without safer, cheaper, U.S.-made batteries, the AI boom hits a wall.
Next Thing Technologies is building the infrastructure that could make it possible.1
Already, **7,800+ investors have committed more than $7 million. 3** And right now, [shares are available at just ](https://substack.com/redirect/74304ecf-9205-40a8-a30c-f2f4938b159f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[$6 with up to 20% in bonus shares.4](https://substack.com/redirect/74304ecf-9205-40a8-a30c-f2f4938b159f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[👉 ](https://substack.com/redirect/74304ecf-9205-40a8-a30c-f2f4938b159f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[Click here to invest in the power behind the AI revolution.](https://substack.com/redirect/74304ecf-9205-40a8-a30c-f2f4938b159f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[ Learn More](https://substack.com/redirect/74304ecf-9205-40a8-a30c-f2f4938b159f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
* * *
## Are we witnessing the birth of a transformative industry or the inflation of a dangerous bubble?
The AI industry's meteoric rise feels both exhilarating and unnervingly familiar. AI Backbone companies like OpenAI, Nvidia, and Oracle are betting billions on a future where artificial intelligence reshapes entire economies and societies. Yet, beneath the glossy projections, a circular economy could be emerging--one where tech giants cycle investment deals among themselves, and where valuations are based on promises of growth, that for now, remain speculative.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/4fe245e8-a5b4-4b6e-85d4-67e9bf9fc004?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
In certain ways this dynamic mirrors the dot-com boom, when companies like Nortel Networks soared on the hype of future internet infrastructure only to collapse when the math didn't add up. Nortel's market cap peaked at $398 billion in 2000, **accounting for a third of Canada 's stock market,** before **[plummeting](https://substack.com/redirect/2d3f58e0-6880-429a-8ca9-df538720d9b8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** to near-zero by 2002.
The brutal lesson was clear: unchecked optimism, fueled by tantalizing theoretical profits, can lead to catastrophic reckonings.
Analyst's have projected that generative AI could add $2.6 trillion to $4.4 trillion to the global economy by 2030, but only if companies can translate adoption into scalable revenue models.
OpenAI's valuation has soared. From $29 Billion in 2023 to its valuation **[this week of $500 Billion](https://substack.com/redirect/4157eb91-a666-42d7-9bb0-0233b6434861?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** making it the **[most valuable privately held company ever.](https://substack.com/redirect/58bea684-8627-48d5-8ff9-241ea7d8b37f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/7605b71a-4161-4fe8-96a6-5dca8167d8cd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
In light of the above table, consider OpenAI's deal with Oracle. The $60 billion figure is eye-popping, but OpenAI's revenue streams--primarily from subscriptions and enterprise licensing--fall far short of justifying such a commitment. Despite ChatGPT's global ubiquity, with millions using AI tools daily, the raw monetization of generative AI, for the moment, remains embryonic. This is not to say that it won't grow to eventually fund that $60 Billion a year annual deal with Oracle but right now its embryonic.
This disconnect raises a question: how sustainable (or stable) is a business model built on promises rather than profits?
Nvidia, another pillar of the AI boom, exemplifies this paradox. The company's chips power the data centers driving AI innovation, and **[its revenue has soared.](https://substack.com/redirect/e5358e2c-dcf4-44b9-8753-6bc4441a171a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[ ](https://substack.com/redirect/e5358e2c-dcf4-44b9-8753-6bc4441a171a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)Yet for sometime now, **[whispers](https://substack.com/redirect/f781a37c-6b7a-45c8-960e-40f6e592a065?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** surround its sales. Industry analysts, such as **[@JustDario](https://substack.com/redirect/0b58ee1c-9d52-4e7e-ac2d-0cbe58c84fac?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** on X, have questioned who exactly is **[buying these chips](https://substack.com/redirect/36ecda7a-7c01-4f13-802f-f1d319a9f07a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** and **[how those deals are structured](https://substack.com/redirect/258080ef-0237-43eb-b539-5e2c975d0483?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**.
These **[details are murky](https://substack.com/redirect/1cb3fc4e-3456-4723-9060-5bbcc9fb49f3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** at best and in some cases **[downright opaque](https://substack.com/redirect/dd8ed261-c74d-4e10-ac98-aecfa397b5e3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**. Are tech giants stockpiling hardware in anticipation of demand, or is this a game of pass-the-debt, where **[purchases are financed](https://substack.com/redirect/7e0e36d8-c96d-4534-8161-de97afc45f3b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** by funds borrowed against inflated valuations?
This lack of transparency harks back to the dot-com era's frenzy, when companies like Pets.com burned through cash on unproven business models, only to fold when investors demanded results.
* * *
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2b27df5b-cf68-4a8f-95c5-987c9544cdbb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
# Or not?
The scale of OpenAI and Oracle's ambitions are most starkly illustrated **[by its energy demands](https://substack.com/redirect/52cf5d3c-28e8-418f-9d9b-fb077cca1f30?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** and it is only when those numbers are examined that the scale of the task ahead rears its head.
* Oracle's proposed data centers would require 4.5 gigawatts of power, enough to supply power to 3 million homes.
* For context, Cembalest references the **[Hoover Dam](https://substack.com/redirect/b513093e-e9e4-4df8-9ec7-43e84b36ecf6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , which powers 1.3 million homes, generates roughly 2 gigawatts annually (depending on water levels) and cost $900 million (in 2025 dollars) to build in the 1930s.
* Replicating that today, adjusted for modern costs, environmental regulations, and geographic suitability would be a multibillion-dollar endeavor.

Nuclear power offers a more realistic solution, but even that is daunting.
* The **[Vogtle Electric](https://substack.com/redirect/122bfb10-d543-4386-83e8-0a81d1ad15a3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** Generating Plant's recently activated Units 3 and 4 in Georgia, which cost $37 billion and together they produce 3.4 gigawatts.
* To meet Oracle's needs, an additional reactor would be needed, conservatively pushing costs toward $55 billion just to build the energy infrastructure required to feed the datacenter.
* $55B would be before accounting for building the data centers themselves or the chips to fill them.

Perhaps the power stations will be funded by the federal government which will defray the cost, but Government intervention will bring inevitable oversight and delays as it is no small thing to put up three nuclear reactors. Also, there are already questions being asked about the percentage of public state electricity consumed by data centers.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/144fa260-e62e-4f17-9928-a40cbb37193d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
With nearly 40% of Virginia's power now being routed to data centers, this very quickly is going to become a political issue.
* * *
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/b5b6676b-1170-483c-9176-8628221a869f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
# No seriously, show me the money?
Debt is the undercurrent that is pulling this enterprise into uncharted waters. Oracle's debt-to-equity ratio, already orders of magnitude higher than Amazon's, signals a company stretching its financial limits. OpenAI, too, despite its latest valuation which is staggering, is banking on a future where its they becomes the Google or Apple of AI for the next quarter-century.
Sam Altman's vision is certainly bold: If OpenAI truly can deliver the world's most advanced AI, today's investments will look like pocket change and he will rightfully go down in history as one of humanity's greats alongside Jobs, Tesla, Edison and Newton.
But the scale of borrowing--coupled with an as yet unproven revenue model--invites comparisons to Enron, which infamously booked theoretical future profits before they had been made (granted this was done with the complicity of their auditor Arthur Andersen.)
While no one accuses OpenAI or Oracle of Enron's criminality, their plan's total reliance on _speculative_ _gains_ raises red flags. Cembalest's quote suggests that even titans of Wall Street's are starting to feel skeptical about the viability of these grand plans.
Even when an industry is mature, titans can fall. Blackberry was once the ultimate communicator but was blown to dust by the iPhone, and the AI "market" is no where near as mature as the consumer cellular market was in 2007.
The AI boom's parallels to the dot-com era are not just financial but cultural. In the late 1990s, the internet was hailed as a transformative force, much as AI is today. Yet, the gap between vision and eventual execution proved fatal for many early companies. Nortel's collapse left thousands jobless and investors ruined, a cautionary tale of what happens when hype outpaces reality.
Today's AI enthusiasts argue that the technology's potential--its ability to revolutionize healthcare, education, and industry--justifies the risk. But potential is not profit, and the logistical hurdles of scaling AI are gargantuan. From power generation to chip supply chains, the infrastructure required demands not just capital but time, political will, and environmental trade-offs.
The AI industry could indeed reshape the world eventually, **[Jeff Bezos certainly thinks so,](https://substack.com/redirect/3770def1-b0e6-466a-9dbe-5cc7532466f5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** delivering fantastical breakthroughs that entirely justify today's gambles. But the path forward requires sobriety, not exuberance. Policymakers must grapple with the environmental and economic implications of AI's growth, while investors should demand transparency in how deals are structured and funded. For the public, the challenge is to separate genuine innovation from speculative froth. The dot-com bust taught us that not every revolution delivers on its initial promises, even if they do in the end.
As we stand on the cusp of AI's next phase, the question looms: are we building a new era of progress, or chasing shadows of a boom that cannot last?
* * *

clean_body: A golden future or re of the Dot-Com Bust? | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? A golden future or re of the Dot-Com Bust? | | | Oct 4 | | | | | | | | | | | | In the late summer of 2025, Oracle's stock surged 25%, propelled by a single announcement: a $60 billion annual deal to provide cloud computing for OpenAI, the creator of ChatGPT. The catch? | | ---|---|--- Michael Cembalest, the Chairman of Market and Investment Strategy for J.P. Morgan Asset & Wealth Management encapsulated it in a nutshell. | | ---|---|--- " Oracle's stock jumped by 25% after being promised $60 billion a year from OpenAI, an amount of money OpenAI doesn't earn yet, to provide cloud computing facilities that Oracle hasn't built yet, and which will require 4.5 GW of power (the equivalent of 2.25 Hoover Dams or four nuclear plants), as well as increased borrowing by Oracle whose debt to equity ratio is already 500% compared to 50% for Amazon, 30% for Microsoft and even less at Meta and Google. In other words, the tech capital cycle may be about to change. " Cembalest's comment that this deal signals a shift in the tech capital cycle is one that eerily recalls the speculative fervor of the dot-com era, a warning of sorts. | | ---|---|--- NVIDIA shocked Wall Street again with $46.74 billion in revenue , up 56% year over year. Their CFO now expects$3 -$4 trillion in AI infrastructure spending by 2030.1 Oracle's results tell the same story. The company reported$14.9 billion in revenue, up 12% year over year. Cloud infrastructure sales jumped55% , while long-term cloud contracts surged to$455 billion -- a 359% increase in just one year. The message couldn't be clearer: AI isn 't a bubble. It's the new economy.1 The Problem No One Talks About: Data centers and AI supercomputers run 24/7, devouring electricity. But lithium batteries can 't keep up.1 They're too expensive, supplies are tight, andChina controls more than 80% of global lithium processing. If AI is going to scale to trillions of dollars, the real constraint isn't chips... It's power storage.1 The Breakthrough That Could Change Everything That's why sodium-ion batteries are emerging as the solution.1 Sodium is1,400x more abundant than lithium , with significant reserves located in the U.S9 Batteries can be made at a fraction of the cost of lithium -- up to 90% less expensive in the first year of use.6 They last longer8, are safer7, and can be built at scale using domestic materials.1 And Next Thing Technologies , an American-owned company, is bringing this breakthrough to market. Their sodium-ion batteries are designed to power everything from massive AI data centers to affordable home backup systems.1 Think about it: NVIDIA builds the chips. Oracle runs the cloud. But without safer, cheaper, U.S.-made batteries, the AI boom hits a wall. Next Thing Technologies is building the infrastructure that could make it possible.1 Already, 7,800+ investors have committed more than $7 million. 3 And right now, Are we witnessing the birth of a transformative industry or the inflation of a dangerous bubble? The AI industry's meteoric rise feels both exhilarating and unnervingly familiar. AI Backbone companies like OpenAI, Nvidia, and Oracle are betting billions on a future where artificial intelligence reshapes entire economies and societies. Yet, beneath the glossy projections, a circular economy could be emerging--one where tech giants cycle investment deals among themselves, and where valuations are based on promises of growth, that for now, remain speculative. | | ---|---|--- In certain ways this dynamic mirrors the dot-com boom, when companies like Nortel Networks soared on the hype of future internet infrastructure only to collapse when the math didn't add up. Nortel's market cap peaked at $398 billion in 2000, accounting for a third of Canada 's stock market, before to near-zero by 2002. The brutal lesson was clear: unchecked optimism, fueled by tantalizing theoretical profits, can lead to catastrophic reckonings. Analyst's have projected that generative AI could add $2.6 trillion to $4.4 trillion to the global economy by 2030, but only if companies can translate adoption into scalable revenue models. OpenAI's valuation has soared. From $29 Billion in 2023 to its valuation making it the | | ---|---|--- In light of the above table, consider OpenAI's deal with Oracle. The $60 billion figure is eye-popping, but OpenAI's revenue streams--primarily from subscriptions and enterprise licensing--fall far short of justifying such a commitment. Despite ChatGPT's global ubiquity, with millions using AI tools daily, the raw monetization of generative AI, for the moment, remains embryonic. This is not to say that it won't grow to eventually fund that $60 Billion a year annual deal with Oracle but right now its embryonic. This disconnect raises a question: how sustainable (or stable) is a business model built on promises rather than profits? Nvidia, another pillar of the AI boom, exemplifies this paradox. The company's chips power the data centers driving AI innovation, and for sometime now, surround its sales. Industry analysts, such as on X, have questioned who exactly is and . These at best and in some cases . Are tech giants stockpiling hardware in anticipation of demand, or is this a game of pass-the-debt, where by funds borrowed against inflated valuations? This lack of transparency harks back to the dot-com era's frenzy, when companies like Pets.com burned through cash on unproven business models, only to fold when investors demanded results. | | ---|---|--- Or not? The scale of OpenAI and Oracle's ambitions are most starkly illustrated and it is only when those numbers are examined that the scale of the task ahead rears its head. Oracle's proposed data centers would require 4.5 gigawatts of power, enough to supply power to 3 million homes. For context, Cembalest references the , which powers 1.3 million homes, generates roughly 2 gigawatts annually (depending on water levels) and cost $900 million (in 2025 dollars) to build in the 1930s. Replicating that today, adjusted for modern costs, environmental regulations, and geographic suitability would be a multibillion-dollar endeavor. Nuclear power offers a more realistic solution, but even that is daunting. The Generating Plant's recently activated Units 3 and 4 in Georgia, which cost $37 billion and together they produce 3.4 gigawatts. To meet Oracle's needs, an additional reactor would be needed, conservatively pushing costs toward $55 billion just to build the energy infrastructure required to feed the datacenter. $55B would be before accounting for building the data centers themselves or the chips to fill them. Perhaps the power stations will be funded by the federal government which will defray the cost, but Government intervention will bring inevitable oversight and delays as it is no small thing to put up three nuclear reactors. Also, there are already questions being asked about the percentage of public state electricity consumed by data centers. | | ---|---|--- With nearly 40% of Virginia's power now being routed to data centers, this very quickly is going to become a political issue. | | ---|---|--- No seriously, show me the money? Debt is the undercurrent that is pulling this enterprise into uncharted waters. Oracle's debt-to-equity ratio, already orders of magnitude higher than Amazon's, signals a company stretching its financial limits. OpenAI, too, despite its latest valuation which is staggering, is banking on a future where its they becomes the Google or Apple of AI for the next quarter-century. But the scale of borrowing--coupled with an as yet unproven revenue model--invites comparisons to Enron, which infamously booked theoretical future profits before they had been made (granted this was done with the complicity of their auditor Arthur Andersen.) Today's AI enthusiasts argue that the technology's potential--its ability to revolutionize healthcare, education, and industry--justifies the risk. But potential is not profit, and the logistical hurdles of scaling AI are gargantuan. Friends, we all know this: government shutdowns are bad for the American economy and the American people.
Millions of people in California and all around the country rely on government services every day, and our economy depends on a functioning federal government.
As we enter the second day of this reckless shutdown, I am strongly urging Congress to pass a funding bill that reopens the government and tackles the health care crisis that Trump’s so-called big, beautiful bill plunged us into.
A huge part of my work since Trump’s second term began has been protecting access to health care. As Trump has tried to dismantle the Affordable Care Act, claw back public health research funding, and illegally cut congressionally appropriated funds from state health programs — we’ve fought back every step of the way.
Now, Congress has a chance to protect health care and lower costs. To refuse to do so at the expense of its own operation is both irresponsible and immoral.

clean_body: One month until we decide on Gov. Newsom’s maps | = | We are working day and night to pass Proposition 50. It is critically important that Gov. Newsom’s ballot measure passes — this is the election to decide whether next year’s midterm elections will be fair or stacked against us. Prop 50 — the Election Rigging Response Act — is our defense against Donald Trump and MAGA Republicans’ gerrymandering scheme to rig Congress in their favor before next year’s elections even begin. We must pass Prop 50. It’s the only way to stop Trump from stealing seats and silencing millions of Californians before ballots are even cast. But we officially only have ONE MONTH until Election Day on November 4th. This campaign has been one of the shortest ballot measure campaigns we’ve ever faced, and Republicans have had the advantage with their dark money donors quickly cutting million-dollar checks while we rely on grassroots supporters like you. = = We hate to be so blunt: But this election to pass Prop is our one shot to stop Donald Trump and extremist Republicans from making an unprecedented power grab to rig next year’s elections before voting even begins. If we don’t pass Prop 50, Donald Trump will have successfully stacked the deck in his favor for 2026. If we fall short on Gov. Newsom’s ballot measure, we will still work relentlessly to flip swing seats from red to blue here in California and retake the U.S. House, but we’ll be starting out far behind. If that happens, Democrats could easily win the “popular vote” for control of the U.S. House, but have no real shot at stopping Donald Trump from another two years of Congressional control. = = = = = = Thank you so much, \- The entire team at the California Democratic Party | | = Our Democratic leaders are working hard to make California a better place to live. From fighting for good-paying jobs, combating climate change, to expanding healthcare access so all Californians can lead safe, healthy, prosperous lives — California’s Democratic leaders turn progressive ideas into meaningful action. name: The Turning Point USA Family
subject: Charlie always believed this day would come
body:
[unsubscribe](https://trk.mg.swampdigest.com/?xul=o5pvyewrzrxe8xiau9slbgewvwn9n8jp0aos6n0rpna599ukpcsfqw2duh8fs92hckpqdk7cpdaktwhs6ednn7ynyrnnxb4l5gntux6&__stmp=t3mai6&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh)
---
Please read the message below from Turning Point USA.
* * *
| | | | [](https://trk.mg.swampdigest.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3mai6&__onlt=h)
---
Friend, Even now... it still doesn't feel real that we lost Charlie. His voice, his courage, his conviction — it shaped all of us. And his vision — **to build the most faithful and the most patriotic generation in American history** — is why Turning Point USA exists today. Friend, that's why we have an inspiring update for you... In just the first seven days since Charlie was taken from all of us, Turning Point USA has received more than 54,000 requests from students who want to start or join a chapter:
---
[](https://trk.mg.swampdigest.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3mai6&__onlt=h)
---
**God is so good.** ✝️🙏🕊️ To give you perspective, Friend... Coming into this school year, Turning Point USA had roughly 900+ college chapters and 1,200+ high school chapters. It took us years to build those chapters... over a decade in fact... _But now?_ These students who signed up? They weren't asked to... They weren't recruited... **They felt it was their time to stand up for this country and honor Charlie.** Because of the life he lived. The faith he had. The love he showed God, his family, and others. The courage he had to speak truth. And his vision of a better America for us all.
---
[](https://trk.mg.swampdigest.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3mai6&__onlt=h)
---
He always believed this day would come. He used to talk about it all the time — how the momentum would shift, how young people would rise up with courage, and how a Turning Point USA chapter would be in every college and high school in America. And now... _It's happening._ Faster than he could have imagined. Bigger than we thought possible. And more beautiful than we can even describe. **Again, Friend, God is so good.** Since we lost Charlie, we've also felt SO MUCH love... Prayers. Emails. Messages. Stories about how Charlie's voice changed lives... or your children's or grandchildren's lives. And so many supporters have asked, _"What can I do?"_ As you read this, our team is working around the clock to follow up with every chapter request. And we're going to need help equipping them with our activism kits. **Every new chapter receives these kits from Turning Point USA with everything they need to get started — including:** ✅ Leadership materials
✅ Pocket Constitutions
✅ Chapter training tools
✅ American flags & patriotic gear Friend, I'm sure you'd believe it when we say it... _this won't be a small task._ But we're going to do it. Whatever it takes. **_For Charlie._** _And here's how YOU can help with this..._ **Every $25 you give today helps stock another activism kit — ready to deploy into a new Turning Point USA student chapter...** How many of these activism kits can you help provide to the wave of new student chapters that are just getting started?
---
[ **1 KIT = $25**](https://trk.mg.swampdigest.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3mai6&__onlt=h)
---
[ **2 KITS = $50**](https://trk.mg.swampdigest.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3mai6&__onlt=h)
---
[ **10 KITS = $250**](https://trk.mg.swampdigest.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3mai6&__onlt=h)
---
[ **100 KITS = $2,500**](https://trk.mg.swampdigest.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3mai6&__onlt=h)
---
[ **1,000 KITS = $25,000**](https://trk.mg.swampdigest.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3mai6&__onlt=h)
---
[ **Sponsor Activism Kits**](https://trk.mg.swampdigest.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3mai6&__onlt=h)
---
Friend, if there was ever a more immediate way you could help make Charlie's vision a reality... **This is it****.**
---
[](https://trk.mg.swampdigest.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3mai6&__onlt=h)
---
Also... **Here's one more powerful thing you can do:** 📩 If you have a son, daughter, grandchild, niece, nephew — or any young person in your life — who wants to get involved... who wants to join or start a chapter... **Please send them this link:** **[TPUSAStudents.com/StartaChapter](https://trk.mg.swampdigest.com/?xtl=edb8w7bac21kwtpess2w4b3qulxzf4336zep2dk72v5ehs5z9ms8dst6levz6816kyfedztncj4aitu9caertgdge8k80taphrfehkbet6ppzm0tshciimfzh4glkwfsvlre60pbk&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3mai6&__onlt=h)** Friend, if you've already given... If you've already donated... If you've already been a voice of encouragement and strength in this painful week... If you've prayed, been praying, and continue to pray... **Thank you.** We can never say it enough. If you've read this far, we want you to know this... **You're the reason Charlie's mission and vision will grow for generations to come.** God is so good.
---
**For Charlie,** -The Turning Point USA Family **P.S.** Please know, Friend, if you can't afford to help right now, all we ask for are your prayers. Charlie would have wanted you to pray first — and if that's all you can give today, _we're grateful._ But if you're able to help... if you're overjoyed to hear that students are flocking to Charlie's message and mission... Your gift today helps sustain this wave of student inspiration and gets new student chapters off the ground floor... And ensures Charlie's vision is realized faster than we all thought possible. **Every $25 you give today helps us stock another activism kit ready to deploy to a new student chapter.**
---
[ **Sponsor an Activism Kit**
**Make Your Gift Here**](https://trk.mg.swampdigest.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3mai6&__onlt=h)
---
Thank you for your help and for your prayers, Friend. **God is so good.** 🙏
---
4940 East Beverly Road
Phoenix, Arizona 85044
---

[Unsubscribe](https://trk.mg.swampdigest.com/?xul=o5pvyewrzrxe8xiau9slbgewvwn9n8jp0aos6n0rpna599ukpcsfqw2duh8fs92hckpqdk7cpdaktwhs6ednn7ynyrnnxb4l5gntux6&__stmp=t3mai6&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh) / [Privacy Policy](https://trk.mg.swampdigest.com/?xtl=qwikcdu20az3577t2f1l5y0v0c2bldcqxcfrtulo944ljrfveibq0a4n0ojdqig186m71rhijw7rg2zq1nw8dktunqqfdtpjkcfkacbdectr0ylap0i267bv12h29awzq5y&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3mai6&__onlt=h) / [Contact us](https://trk.mg.swampdigest.com/?xtl=cyfvpmfkldvqlnbvicb200g7gdu5nglsu6p2xjbx382bkwmkcgs9tgom164h9gkyu74zcsbs8sgnwvxfinpw6jbody36hs6ca9bjtx4fj3a17xffp051c3jf&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3mai6&__onlt=h)

How many of these activism kits can you help provide to the wave of new student chapters that are just getting started? Friend, if there was ever a more immediate way you could help make Charlie's vision a reality... This is it**. Also... Here's one more powerful thing you can do: 📩 If you have a son, daughter, grandchild, niece, nephew — or any young person in your life — who wants to get involved... who wants to join or start a chapter... Please send them this link: Friend, if you've already given... If you've already donated... If you've already been a voice of encouragement and strength in this painful week... If you've prayed, been praying, and continue to pray... Thank you. We can never say it enough. If you've read this far, we want you to know this... You're the reason Charlie's mission and vision will grow for generations to come. God is so good. For Charlie, -The Turning Point USA Family P.S. Please know, Friend, if you can't afford to help right now, all we ask for are your prayers. Charlie would have wanted you to pray first — and if that's all you can give today, we're grateful. But if you're able to help... if you're overjoyed to hear that students are flocking to Charlie's message and mission... Your gift today helps sustain this wave of student inspiration and gets new student chapters off the ground floor... And ensures Charlie's vision is realized faster than we all thought possible. Every $25 you give today helps us stock another activism kit ready to deploy to a new student chapter. Thank you for your help and for your prayers, Friend. God is so good. 🙏 4940 East Beverly Road Phoenix, Arizona 85044 / / AFFILIATE DISCLOSURE NOTICE: You should assume the sender of this message has a financial or affiliate relationship with the persons or companies referenced and may be compensated for purchases made via linked sites. body:
Please let us explain how you can take action today, Peter.
#
There are three ways you can help grind Donald Trump’s hostile takeover of our country to a SCREECHING HALT. Please let us explain:
1. **Make sure you’re registered to vote.** Voting is the most direct way you can influence what happens in your area and at the national level. Please make sure your voter registration is active + updated.
2. **Volunteer for a candidate or cause you care about.** Investing your time + energy is one of the best ways to build connections with your neighbors and make a real difference in your community.
3. **Chip in to help us elect Democrats in all 50 states.** Donations of any amount — whether they’re $1, $10, or $100 — will help us communicate with voters, support candidates across the country, and protect our precious freedoms from the Trump administration’s repeated overreach.

name: Andrew Hayman
subject: Before MAGA reaches them and poisons the well
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY DEFEND OUR COURTS, NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE
body:
We need people to remember the conversation they had with a real person about the truth of the Democrat Justices records, rather than the monsters dark money MAGA PACs are trying to make them out as
| | | | | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001vEnjZS1B-MHknEsmHF-oKo3UB2dMbN7TlG93sqfTaZLlCWcJEljmMQal6-di1h4gxk3SogQj_RFz4FWDguNwn8WQkIvrZAIhsEEGlS5cmX09jA82NIM2S-_tWu-xSXWlyMhaBpsH9e6ntBoiMZG_4HfSUwyq6_0Ju6zVs5zNIHwNrSoZnAr3T_iV6Qn-6Jbv30JPHajML5X9eJ3EaWJTqdNCYWbQImF2LSIaZyDfv24=&c=QpI7Lwi7qia3x9xe46Nr9dCGFF2UbMPPijgJ7A5TPwAZGMTpJIqnug==&ch=D9kh1LWjz_np9tt_KW7jBQvBGLXk4smpps8XwTPd1VrQXP2_4IsMkA==)
---
| Peter,
My name is Andrew Hayman. I am the field director for Defend Our Courts.In case you don't know what a field director does, I oversee and manage the campaigns strategic voter persuasion, voter contact, and get out the vote operations.
The November PA Supreme Court retention election is the most important election of 2025 for Democrats.
If MAGA defeats the 3 Democrats on the court up for retention, they will have control of the body overseeing elections in the most important swing state in the upcoming '26 and '28 elections--allowing them to seize complete control of PA from Dems and maintain a grip on the White House for at least a decade.
In less than four months, Defend Our Courts has solidified itself as one of the most important Democratic organizations working to contain MAGA's surprise breakthrough in this race. Though the situation is dire we've been putting up one hell of a fight:
---
| | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001vEnjZS1B-MHknEsmHF-oKo3UB2dMbN7TlG93sqfTaZLlCWcJEljmMQal6-di1h4gxk3SogQj_RFz4FWDguNwn8WQkIvrZAIhsEEGlS5cmX09jA82NIM2S-_tWu-xSXWlyMhaBpsH9e6ntBoiMZG_4HfSUwyq6_0Ju6zVs5zNIHwNrSoZnAr3T_iV6Qn-6Jbv30JPHajML5X9eJ3EaWJTqQOlMkk_D4IARTU1I1XAwGw=&c=QpI7Lwi7qia3x9xe46Nr9dCGFF2UbMPPijgJ7A5TPwAZGMTpJIqnug==&ch=D9kh1LWjz_np9tt_KW7jBQvBGLXk4smpps8XwTPd1VrQXP2_4IsMkA==)
---
Here's what we've accomplished in less than four months:

* Raised over 2 million dollars and have hired over 100+ staff members.

* Deployed thousands of volunteers to knock doors across PA.

* Are regularly hosting canvases that reach over 10,000 doors knocked.

* PA Dems are being saturated with millions of our digital ads, mailers, calls, and text messages.

At the end of the day, most Democrats we talk to still don't know that there is even an election in November. So, the more people we speak to, the more votes we get, which is why we're going all out with texts, door knocks, calls, and digital ads.
And now we also have to worry about the MAGA billionaires targeting Democrats with mailers and digital ads meant to deceive them into voting "no" against the Democrat Justices.
They are telling Democrats that these judges are "against women's rights" and that they "protect pedophiles"--insidious lies meant to confuse Democrats into voting against their own interest as the Justices party affiliation won't appear next to their name on the ballot.
We need voters to remember the conversation they had with a real person about the truth of the Democrat Justices records, rather than the monsters that dark money MAGA PACs are trying to make them out to be.
[We have 31 days until Election Day. This final month will be a high stakes race to reach as many PA Democrats before MAGA reaches them and poisons the well. Can we count on you to rush a donation of $50 or whatever you can spare to help us fuel our final push?](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001vEnjZS1B-MHknEsmHF-oKo3UB2dMbN7TlG93sqfTaZLlCWcJEljmMQal6-di1h4gE9DsUVyWvgeTfgj9vHXens0fDykHXPTSmqzgLAsrFlKjJO0w6YWaDGDWdvF3ZtoVjszRXggLlDeWKOBFjcb1umg8lXurtViSO0-kGzeFE9H8CVILYPSRSZHOHMIRD_UFtWOz0RACXfILn7n_b-rn4YdZ9ZPu-NQnX_ES7AN2OYYklnc9wGa8cA==&c=QpI7Lwi7qia3x9xe46Nr9dCGFF2UbMPPijgJ7A5TPwAZGMTpJIqnug==&ch=D9kh1LWjz_np9tt_KW7jBQvBGLXk4smpps8XwTPd1VrQXP2_4IsMkA==)

name: John Hickenlooper
subject: hanging on by their fingertips

body:
This is a devastating blow to Coloradans

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/116588743/573901679/1435897075?refcode=email_100425_ruralhealthcare&refcode2=sl2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMGY1YzFlYy00NWExLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=7gp5tmu4RDf5ynQtQq73h5yQUVPICCMzK7sgMrfqt8M=&emci=dcf5c1ec-45a1-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=e0f5c1ec-45a1-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ceid=7280715) Folks, rural health care providers in communities across Colorado and the nation are terrified of what’s coming down the line from the Republican budget — or the “Big Beautiful Betrayal” as we like to call it. Right now, rural health care providers and nursing homes are hanging on by their fingertips, working to scrape together the resources they need to stay open and keep providing for their patients as Trump and the Republicans’ massive cuts are looming on the horizon. **In the coming years, 15 MILLION Americans could lose access to their Medicaid coverage.** That means millions of people won’t be able to get the health care they need AND our rural clinics and nursing homes won’t be able to earn the funds needed to stay afloat. Millions more will lose their protections against “pre-existing medical conditions.” And Republicans voted to rip these critical resources away from clinics and working families for what? Just to pay for a lavish tax handout to the wealthiest Americans? _Gimme a break._ Look, we’re fighting tooth and nail to restore this vital funding and keep these clinics open because slashing health care funding for millions of people — _in the wealthiest country in the world, no less_ — doesn’t make a lick of sense. [_**We’re never going to stop fighting for working Americans, and that’s why I need your support today. Please, can you pitch in a couple bucks to help us keep up the fight for restoring this critical health care funding?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/116588745/573901681/1531865432?amount=15&refcode=email_100425_ruralhealthcare&refcode2=sl2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMGY1YzFlYy00NWExLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=7gp5tmu4RDf5ynQtQq73h5yQUVPICCMzK7sgMrfqt8M=) | | [DONATE →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/116588748/573901683/1531865432?amount=15&refcode=email_100425_ruralhealthcare&refcode2=sl2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMGY1YzFlYy00NWExLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=7gp5tmu4RDf5ynQtQq73h5yQUVPICCMzK7sgMrfqt8M=)

subject: AIPAC is using its multi-million dollar war chest to control our democracy

body:
[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/30?refcode=em20251004-1041&t=1001&akid=1052%2E251025%2EtSLPRP ]Abdul for US Senate [ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/30?refcode=em20251004-1041&t=1002&akid=1052%2E251025%2EtSLPRP ]Abdul for US Senate

Peter, this
is what we’re up against:

[ POLITICO: ]POLITICO: AIPAC is biggest source of Republican donors giving to
Democratic primaries

AIPAC is using its multi-million dollar war chest to control our
democracy.

When certain politicians refuse to condemn Netanyahu’s genocide and look
the other way, or worse, enable the needless war by voting to spend our
tax dollars on bombs overseas, this is why.

AIPAC bankrolls campaigns that stay complicit, and targets campaigns that
speak out, including ours.

Meanwhile, people across this country — Democrats, independents, and even
many Republicans — want the carnage to end.

No one wants to keep seeing pictures of children starving, families torn
apart, and entire neighborhoods flattened.

That’s why our campaign has never pandered to AIPAC, and never will. We’re
the only campaign in this race who can say that.

But because we’re sticking to our values, AIPAC is coming after us with
full force.

They’ve already begun fundraising in support of our primary opponent, and
with the primary potentially coming up three months sooner than we
thought, we have to get our message in front of voters before AIPAC
reaches them.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-ms-aes-20251004-1-ak?amount=10&refcode=em20251004-1041&refcode2=1052_251025_tSLPRP&akid=1052%2E251025%2EtSLPRP ]Can you fuel our work by pitching in $10 today?

Last cycle, AIPAC spent over $100 million to take down campaigns like
ours. The only way we can overcome their attacks is by sticking with each
other, Peter.

Together, we’ll prove that integrity and courage are stronger than AIPAC’s
millions.

Thank you,

Team Abdul
